navigate_to.name: Navigate to Coordinates
navigate_to.description: Pathfinds and moves to target coordinates using BFS step-by-step. Automatically stops if the game enters battle or dialog modes. Args: x (int), y (int), max_steps (int, optional).

code.old:
import collections

target_x = args.get('x')
target_y = args.get('y')
max_steps = args.get('max_steps', 50)

def get_path(grid, start_x, start_y, tx, ty):
    queue = collections.deque([(start_x, start_y, [])])
    visited = set([(start_x, start_y)])
    while queue:
        x, y, path = queue.popleft()
        if x == tx and y == ty:
            return path
        for dx, dy, btn in [(0, -1, 'UP'), (0, 1, 'DOWN'), (-1, 0, 'LEFT'), (1, 0, 'RIGHT')]:
            nx, ny = x + dx, y + dy
            if 0 <= ny < len(grid) and 0 <= nx < len(grid[0]):
                tile = grid[ny][nx]
                is_walkable = tile in ['.', '~', 'D', 'I']
                if not is_walkable:
                    if tile == '↓' and btn == 'DOWN': is_walkable = True
                    if tile == '←' and btn == 'LEFT': is_walkable = True
                    if tile == '→' and btn == 'RIGHT': is_walkable = True
                if is_walkable and (nx, ny) not in visited:
                    visited.add((nx, ny))
                    queue.append((nx, ny, path + [btn]))
    return None

steps = 0
final_pos = None
while steps < max_steps:
    state = tools['get_game_state']()
    if 'overworld' not in state.get('state_text', '').lower():
        break
    
    data = tools['get_map_data']()
    grid = data['grid']
    pos = data['player']
    final_pos = (pos['x'], pos['y'])
    
    if pos['x'] == target_x and pos['y'] == target_y:
        break
        
    path = get_path(grid, pos['x'], pos['y'], target_x, target_y)
    if not path:
        print(f"No path found to {target_x}, {target_y}")
        break
        
    btn = path[0]
    tools['press_buttons'](buttons=[btn], reasoning=f"Step {steps+1}/{max_steps}: moving {btn} towards ({target_x},{target_y})")
    steps += 1

result = {'steps_taken': steps, 'final_pos': final_pos}

navigate_to.title: Navigate to Coordinates

walk_to.name: Walk To Coordinates
walk_to.description: Pathfinds and moves to target coordinates using BFS step-by-step. Automatically stops if the game enters battle or dialog modes. Args: x (int), y (int), max_steps (int, optional).

code.old:
import collections

target_x = args.get('x')
target_y = args.get('y')
max_steps = args.get('max_steps', 25)

def get_path(grid, start_x, start_y, tx, ty):
    queue = collections.deque([(start_x, start_y, [])])
    visited = set([(start_x, start_y)])
    while queue:
        x, y, path = queue.popleft()
        if x == tx and y == ty:
            return path
        for dx, dy, btn in [(0, -1, 'UP'), (0, 1, 'DOWN'), (-1, 0, 'LEFT'), (1, 0, 'RIGHT')]:
            nx, ny = x + dx, y + dy
            if 0 <= ny < len(grid) and 0 <= nx < len(grid[0]):
                tile = grid[ny][nx]
                is_walkable = tile in ['.', '~', 'D', 'I']
                if tile == '↓' and btn == 'DOWN': is_walkable = True
                if tile == '←' and btn == 'LEFT': is_walkable = True
                if tile == '→' and btn == 'RIGHT': is_walkable = True
                if is_walkable and (nx, ny) not in visited:
                    visited.add((nx, ny))
                    queue.append((nx, ny, path + [btn]))
    return None

steps = 0
final_pos = None
while steps < max_steps:
    state = tools['get_game_state']()
    if 'overworld' not in state.get('state_text', '').lower():
        break
        
    data = tools['get_map_data']()
    grid = data['grid']
    pos = data['player']
    final_pos = (pos['x'], pos['y'])
    
    if pos['x'] == target_x and pos['y'] == target_y:
        break
        
    path = get_path(grid, pos['x'], pos['y'], target_x, target_y)
    if not path:
        print(f"No path found to {target_x}, {target_y}")
        break
        
    btn = path[0]
    tools['press_buttons'](buttons=[btn], reasoning=f"Step {steps+1}/{max_steps}: moving {btn} towards ({target_x},{target_y})")
    steps += 1

result = {'steps_taken': steps, 'final_pos': final_pos}

bfs_move.name: BFS Move To Coords
bfs_move.description: Obstacle-aware BFS pathfinding that handles random encounters by mashing A. Use this for all long-distance travel, especially in caves. Args: x (int), y (int), max_moves (int, default 80).

description.old: Navigates to {x, y} coordinates using BFS to avoid obstacles and doors. Pass 'x', 'y', and optionally 'max_steps'.
description.new: Obstacle-aware BFS pathfinding that handles random encounters by mashing A. Use this for all long-distance travel, especially in caves. Args: x (int), y (int), max_moves (int, default 80).

bfs_move.title: BFS Move To Coords

safe_navigate.description: Safe BFS navigation to x and y coords. Args: x, y, max_steps

cave_bfs.name: Cave BFS
cave_bfs.description: Obstacle-aware BFS pathfinding that robustly handles random cave encounters by automatically mashing A. Args: x (int), y (int), max_moves (int).
cave_bfs.title: Cave BFS

smart_cave_bfs.name: Smart Cave BFS
smart_cave_bfs.description: Obstacle-aware BFS pathfinding that robustly handles random cave encounters by automatically mashing A. Parses state_text for the grid instead of get_map_data(). Args: x (int), y (int), max_moves (int).
smart_cave_bfs.title: Smart Cave BFS

cave_router.name: Cave Router BFS
cave_router.description: Pathfinds safely through caves by detecting and managing wild battles with mashing A, while routing around NPCs and walls. Args: x (int), y (int).
cave_router.title: Cave Router BFS

smart_navigate.name: Smart Navigate
smart_navigate.description: Obstacle-aware BFS pathfinding that executes actions. Safely handles ledges and grass. Aborts on battle or if stuck.
smart_navigate.title: Smart Navigate

city_nav.name: City Navigator
city_nav.description: BFS based obstacle avoiding navigation for towns and cities.
city_nav.title: City Navigator
